title: EFL Latest: Frank Lampard, Alex Neil and Kim Hellberg Nominated for Manager of the Year in End of Season Awards
text: The English Football League has unveiled its nominations for the 2026 EFL Awards, with the ceremony taking place live on Sky Sports Football on Sunday, April 19 at 8:30pm. Championship Manager of the Season: Four managers are competing for the Championship Manager of the Season award. Frank Lampard of Coventry City leads the shortlist after guiding his side to the top of the table. He faces competition from Alex Neil (Millwall), Sergej Jakirovic (Hull City), and Kim Hellberg (Middlesbrough), whose teams remain in contention for promotion as the season approaches its climax. Championship Player of the Season: The Championship Player of the Season award features goalkeeper Carl Rushworth (Coventry City), midfielder Hayden Hackney (Middlesbrough), winger Femi Azeez (Millwall), and striker Zan Vipotnik (Swansea City), who is the division's current top scorer with 20 goals. League One: Michael Skubala has already secured promotion for Lincoln City and is nominated alongside Graham Alexander (Bradford City), Brian Barry-Murphy (Cardiff City), and Alex Revell (Stevenage) for League One Manager of the Season. The League One Player of the Season nominees include Dom Ballard (Leyton Orient), Owen Bailey (Doncaster Rovers), Jack Moylan (Lincoln City), and Sonny Bradley (Lincoln City). League Two: Andy Woodman (Bromley), Paul Warne (Milton Keynes Dons), Micky Mellon (Oldham Athletic), and Neil Harris (Cambridge United) are shortlisted for League Two Manager of the Season. League Two Player of the Season contenders are Aaron Drinan (Swindon Town), Mitch Pinnock (Bromley), Callum Paterson (Milton Keynes Dons), and Omar Sowumni (Bromley). Additional Awards: The ceremony will also recognize young players across all divisions, community contributions, and off-pitch achievements. The prestigious Sir Tom Finney Award and Contribution to League Football Award will be announced on the night.
